resolutions.errorMessage: get_material_expressions not found on MaterialEditingLibrary — duplicate expression nodes accumulate
resolutions.developerIntent: Clean up duplicate material expression nodes before creating new ones

attemptedFixes.action: Use mel.get_material_expressions(mat) to iterate and delete
attemptedFixes.notes: Method does not exist on MaterialEditingLibrary
get_num_material_expressions returns count, delete_all_material_expressions removes all. Then recreate only needed nodes.

successfulFix.description: Use delete_all_material_expressions(mat) to clear all nodes, then recreate only the needed ones. There is no getter for all expressions — use get_num_material_expressions to check count.
steps: Call mel.get_num_material_expressions(mat) to check if nodes exist
Recreate only the needed expression nodes with mel.create_material_expression()

resolutions.rootCause: MaterialEditingLibrary has no get_material_expressions() method to list all expressions. The correct cleanup API is delete_all_material_expressions() which removes everything. Always call this before creating new expressions to prevent duplicate accumulation.
